content.full_text:
August 31, 1976

Mr. William J. Hemby
California Organization of Police
and Sheriffs
921 Eleventh Street, Suite 703
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Hemby:

Thank you for your letter of August 26, concerning your organization's efforts in establishing a statewide political action committee.

I will respond to your questions in the order they were presented.

1. The Political Reform Act does not prohibit or place any restriction on an association's use of money collected from individual dues for the making of political contributions.

2. If local member associations of COPS-PAC receive funds from members earmarked for a political purpose (see attached PPPC Regulations 18215 and 18225) and they forward those funds along with money received from dues to the statewide political action committee for the purpose of making political contributions, but do not themselves make the contributions, the local association will have no reporting requirements.

3. There is no limitation on the amount of money which may be contributed to a candidate or committee.

4. There are no restrictions under the Political Reform Act concerning the method by which COPS-PAC receives its funds from local associations for the purpose of making political contributions.

5. Federal political action guidelines apply only to federal candidates and their committees.

I hope the above answers clarify any questions you have concerning the establishment of your political action committee. If I may be of additional assistance, Please do not hesitate to let me know.

Very truly yours,

REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
RSK:mak                                Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

bcc: Jeanne

[The image shows a mostly blank page with two gray circular marks near the top and some faint handwritten marks in the center and upper right corner that are not clearly legible]

California Organization of Police and Sheriffs
(11TH & J BUILDING)
921 11TH STREET, SUITE 703
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 441-3336
TOLL FREE 800-952-5282

August 26, 1976

Ms. Regina Kutchins
Technical Assistance Coordinator
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Kutchins:

The California Organization of Police & Sheriffs is interested in
establishing a statewide Political Action Committee.

The role of the COPS-PAC would be to make outright donations to
candidates running for state offices (i.e., Assembly, Senate and Constitutional offices); to contribute to political fund raising dinners and
other functions; to contribute to state constitutional amendments and
possibly local ballot measures.

Certain questions concerning the legality of using funds from our
local associations has slowed the creation of a COPS-PAC. Your assistance
in answering the following would help us considerably.

(1) Can local police and deputy sheriffs associations donate moneys
collected from individual dues to COPS-PAC?

(2) If all contributions for the above mentioned reasons, including
contributions to local races comes from COPS-PAC, are local member associations still required to file as a committee; i.e., if our locals cease
all donations for political purposes?

(3) Is there a ceiling on the amount of contributions to a candidate?

(4) Do donations to COPS-PAC have to be voluntarily contributed from
local associations; i.e., could COPS include an additional amount of dues
to fund its Political Action Committee?

(5) Do federal political action guidelines, as far as contributions
to candidates are concerned, also apply to how funds are raised and contributed in state campaigns?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter. COPS is determined to
live within the guidelines of the Fair Political Practices Commission

Ms. Regina Kutchins
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Page Two, August 26, 1976


and will seek your help in the forming of our Political Action Committee.

Again, Ms. Kutchins, many thanks, and I look forward to hearing from
you soon.

Sincerely,

C.O.P.S.

William G. Hemby
Executive Secretary

WJH:br
